assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the reason that very few bronze sculptures from ancient Greece exist today? **

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Bronze was the preferred medium for freestanding sculpture in ancient Greece.
Because metal can be melted and recast as coins, weapons, and other metal goods, many bronze sculptures from antiquity were destroyed and reused for other purposes.

choices.content: The metal was melted and used for other purposes.
The Romans replaced the Greek originals with their own marble reproductions.
Bronze was expensive and rarely used by Greek artists.
Bronze sculpture is not durable over time.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did Hellenistic artists add details such as the inlaid eyes, use of inset copper, and incisions to the cast bronze in this sculpture?**   

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/da477f032f67b4d5cbee34128a144690.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: In the Hellenistic period, artists shifted their interest away from the idealized forms of the Classical period.
Details such as the eyes, inset copper, and incisions in the bronze contribute to the life-like quality of this sculpture and highlight both physical and psychological suffering in the man's life.

choices.content: to enhance the sculpture's life-like quality
to increase the sculpture's economic value
to underscore the figure's non-Greek features
to create an idealized form typical of the Hellenistic period

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which feature most recalls the idealized forms of Classical sculpture?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6b84a65d04ceeb34b3293f8bd8ad3c27.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: While the collapsed posture, solemn expression, and sense of engagement suggest weariness in the figure, his muscular torso and legs recall the idealized athletic body of Classical sculpture.

choices.content: The man's muscles and athletic physique
The man's turned head and implied interaction with others
The man's facial expression and restrained emotion
The man's seated posture and clasped hands

assessment_items.question_content:
**The shift toward new subject matter in the Hellenistic period reflects the notion that beauty might be found in $\_\_\_\_\_\_$ .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The *Seated Boxer* exhibits an interest in human pathos, reflecting the Hellenistic notion that beauty might be found in all life experience.

choices.content: life experience
physical perfection
nature and the environment
intellectual understanding
mathematical precision

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which detail indicates this figure's profession?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6b84a65d04ceeb34b3293f8bd8ad3c27.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: In ancient Greece, boxing focused on blows to the head and face of one's opponent.   Details such as the figure's broken nose and swollen ear, the open gashes on his face, and the bandages on his bloody hands reveal what the sport has done to the boxer's body.

choices.content: The broken nose
The bandaged hands
The slumped shoulders
The lifted foot
The swollen ear
